title: Subjectivity in Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Subjectivity in Evangelion             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Jun 27, 2014 2:53 am I swear this is a theme. "There are as many truths as there are people. But there is only one truth that is your truth. That's the one that's formed by whatever point of view that you chose to view it from. It's a revised perception that protects you." Could someone expla--no, actually, could someone link me to an essay on this? I know there is one. Evangelion has a pretty big following, to the say the least. Oh, wait, this is a forum. So, uh, discuss this amongst yourselves? Man, it'd be nice if I just had one person who was really into Eva and could explain all of this to me.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Fri Jun 27, 2014 4:10 am ^ Bro, it's all up to your personal interpretation, if you need to know what the majority thinks you should check out the wiki, it has a lot helpful articles

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jun 27, 2014 8:01 am I'll take a stab at it. Essentially that's getting at the fact that nobody has perfect information (and even then people don't necessarily cares about all the information available), so we understand and judge the world, events, and people from a specific point of view that is tailored around what we're doing and care about. This point of view gives narrative meaning to events that would from an objective view be simple facts by transforming them into setting, obstacles, tools and so forth in the story of our lives. It's not just a bundle of atoms and forces, it's a car... it's MY car... and that's why it matters to me that your car just altered the arrangement of atoms and forces in my car in a way I didn't desire, as well as why I don't care that it happened because there was a bee trying to sting you in the eye though that may very well be an important fascet of the experience for you. This 'protects you' in that it gives context and meaning to the story of your own existence in a world that fundamentally doesn't give a shit about you, your choices or aspirations, thus shielding you from existential oblivion and nihilism. If something matters to you then it has value even if nobody else notices or appreciates it, nor will it stop having value for you now just because it won't last forever.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Jun 28, 2014 3:06 am Woah, Nem, that's the best explanation I've ever heard for the "anything matters if you think it matters" argument. Thank you. ChaddyManPrime wrote: ^ Bro, it's all up to your personal interpretation, if you need to know what the majority thinks you should check out the wiki, it has a lot helpful articles
